metadata.note: 40 gdoc + 35 gsheet train chunks. 24 regular test queries are paraphrases of specific train entries; 1 no-evidence query is designed to return nothing above MIN_SIMILARITY=0.70.

train.content: The venue for the Winter Formal 2026 was Aria Banquet Hall on King Street West. Room rental cost: $1,800 for the evening, including AV setup and coat-check service.
End-of-year celebration catering was ordered from Campus Catering Co. for $750, covering appetizers, a dessert station, and non-alcoholic beverages for 80 attendees.
The fall networking night was held at the Faculty of Business atrium, Room 230. Maximum capacity 60 people. Booking made via the university room reservation portal two weeks in advance.
Reimbursement requests must include an itemized receipt, expense category, and written approval from the VP Finance. Requests submitted more than 30 days after the expense will not be processed.

VP Finance for 2025-2026: Jordan Lee (jordan.lee@university.edu). Responsibilities: budget tracking, processing reimbursements, managing sponsorship invoices, and presenting monthly financial reports.

Club brand colours: Navy Blue (#1A2B5E) and Gold (#C9A84C). Official font: Montserrat Bold for headers, Lato Regular for body. All logo files are stored in the Google Drive brand kit folder. Do not alter colours or fonts without a majority exec vote.

Spring Kickoff 2026 post-event summary: 94 students attended. Average feedback score: 4.3/5. Total net cost: $1,050 (venue $600, catering $350, printing $100). Recommended venue for next year: same location.

Approved platforms: Notion for internal documentation, Google Drive for file storage, Slack for team communication, Eventbrite for event registrations, and Canva Pro for design assets. Personal accounts must not be used for club business.
Semester budget allocation approved at AGM: Events $3,200, Marketing $600, Workshops $800, Team-building $400, Emergency Reserve $500. Total approved annual budget: $5,500.

New exec onboarding steps: (1) shadow outgoing exec for two weeks, (2) complete the university financial accountability training module, (3) read the Club Constitution in full, (4) set up Notion and Slack access, (5) meet with the club advisor within the first month.

Year-end fund policy: any unspent funds are carried over to the next executive team's budget. Surplus funds cannot be redistributed to members or used for personal expenses. The carry-over amount must be disclosed in the handover document.
Venue confirmed for Annual Gala 2026: The Grand Hall at 45 University Ave. Capacity: 200 guests. Rental fee: $2,400. Event date: March 28, 2026. Includes round tables, linens, and basic lighting.

Speaker bios for Finance Careers Night: Priya Singh (CFO, Meridian Capital), David Osei (Portfolio Manager, TGF Investments), Amanda Wu (VP Finance, Regional Bank). All speakers have agreed to a 20-minute presentation and Q&A.
Constitution Article 4 — Quorum: A quorum of 50% of voting members plus one must be present for any constitutional amendment. General resolutions require a simple majority of those present.

Sponsorship tiers for 2025-2026: Bronze ($250 — logo on website), Silver ($500 — logo + social shoutout), Gold ($1,000 — logo + social + speaking slot), Platinum ($2,000+ — full naming rights for one event).

The club's Instagram account (@bizclub_uni) is managed by the VP Communications. Posting frequency: minimum three times per week. All posts must be approved by the VP Communications before going live.

Mentorship program structure: each mentor (2nd year or above) is matched with one mentee (1st year). Commitment: 4 meetings per semester. Mentors are expected to help with course selection, club involvement, and career direction.
The club partners with the university Career Centre to run two resume-review workshops per semester. Workshops are limited to 25 attendees. Booking opens one week before the event via Eventbrite.

Meeting minutes — November 12, 2025: Approved $200 for case competition prep materials. Voted 7-2 to invite alumni panel for spring event. Deferred decision on new membership fee structure to next meeting.

End-of-term handover checklist: transfer Notion ownership, add new exec to Google Drive, update Slack admin list, change Eventbrite account email, submit updated club registration with student union, archive old documents.
The club's emergency reserve fund currently holds $500. It may only be drawn on with unanimous exec approval and must be replenished within the same fiscal year if used.

Accessibility policy for events: all club events must be held in wheelchair-accessible venues. Presenters must provide slide decks in advance for translation. ASL interpretation available on request with two weeks notice.
VP Events for 2025-2026: Marcus Johnson. Responsibilities include booking venues, coordinating catering, managing event registration, and conducting post-event surveys. Reports to the President.

The annual leadership retreat for exec members is held at a rented cottage north of the city each September. Attendance is mandatory for all exec members. Budget: $1,200 covering accommodation, meals, and activities.
The club's official email is bizclub@university.edu. All external correspondence must go through this address. The president and VP Communications share access. Response time target: 48 hours.

Elections are held in March each year. All candidates must have been general members for at least one full semester. Nominations open two weeks before election day. Results announced within 24 hours of voting.

Photography at club events: the club contracts a student photographer at $80/event. Photos are shared in the Google Drive events folder within one week. Members must opt out if they do not consent to being photographed.

The club's fiscal year runs from May 1 to April 30. The VP Finance must submit an audited budget summary to the student union by April 15 each year. Failure to file on time may result in suspension of club funding.

Workshop feedback summary — Q1 2025: Resume Review avg. 4.5/5 (n=24). Finance 101 avg. 4.1/5 (n=19). Case Prep avg. 4.7/5 (n=21). Most common request: more interactive elements and smaller group sizes.

The diversity and inclusion committee is responsible for at least one cultural event per semester. The committee has a budget of $300 per event and reports to the VP Equity. Membership is open to all club members.

Partnerships and MOU: the club has a signed Memorandum of Understanding with the Computer Science Society for co-hosted hackathons. The MOU outlines shared promotion responsibilities and 50/50 cost-splitting.

Code of Conduct: members are expected to act with professionalism at all club events and online. Harassment, discrimination, or misuse of club resources will result in immediate suspension pending review by the executive team.

Meeting minutes — January 20, 2026: Approved gala venue deposit of $800. Voted to extend case competition registration deadline by one week. Discussed Q3 budget review; $1,450 remaining across all categories.

Guest policy: non-members may attend up to two club events as guests before being required to join. Guests must be accompanied by a member and must register in advance. Guest spots are capped at 20% of event capacity.

Post-event debrief template: attendance count, budget variance (actual vs planned), top 3 things that went well, top 3 things to improve, follow-up actions with owners and due dates. Debrief must be filed within 5 business days.

Speaker honorarium policy: external speakers may receive a honorarium up to $500. Honorarium must be approved by the VP Finance at least two weeks in advance. Payment processed via student union cheque within 30 days.

The club newsletter is published every other Tuesday via Mailchimp. Content is curated by the VP Communications and includes industry news, upcoming club events, and a member spotlight. Subscribe link on club website.

Procurement policy: all purchases over $100 must be pre-approved by the VP Finance. Purchases over $500 require full exec approval via a motion at a general meeting. Approved vendors are listed in the Finance Handbook appendix.

Handover document from 2024-2025 to 2025-2026 exec: unspent carry-over balance $342. Open sponsorship negotiation with Alpha Consulting (follow up by October 1). Pending venue refund from winter formal deposit ($200).

Volunteer appreciation event 2026: planned for April 10, casual dinner at The Bistro on campus. Budget: $280 for dinner for 14 volunteers. No formal program — just a thank-you gathering before the academic year ends.

Category: Printing | Description: Recruitment flyers — September intake | Amount: $143.50 | Date: 2025-09-15 | Submitted by: VP Marketing | Status: Reimbursed

Social Media Lead: Lily Zhang | Platforms: Instagram (@bizclub_uni), LinkedIn (BizClub University) | Posting schedule: Monday, Thursday, Saturday | Backup: VP Communications
Budget Remaining — March 2026 snapshot: Events $420, Marketing $180, Workshops $350, Emergency Reserve $500 (untouched). Total unspent: $1,450 of $5,500 allocated.
Membership count as of February 2026: 127 active general members, 14 executive team members. Total: 141. Lapsed memberships (not renewed): 23.
Exec Team Dinner — November 2025: Venue: Restaurant Botanica | Total bill: $312.40 | Club contribution: $10/person × 14 exec = $140 | Remaining $172.40 paid personally | Receipt on file
National Case Competition 2026: Attendees — Aisha Patel, Marcus Johnson | Result: Top 10 finish (out of 42 teams) | Travel cost: $480 (flights + hotel) | Registration fee: $150
Name: Jordan Lee | Role: VP Finance | Email: jlee@uni.edu | Phone: 647-555-0291 | Year: 3rd | Emergency contact: parent 416-555-0900
Category: Catering | Description: Fall mixer appetizers — 60 guests | Amount: $480 | Date: 2025-10-18 | Submitted by: VP Events | Status: Paid
Workshop Sign-Ups — Case Prep Session Feb 21: 1. Aisha Patel, 2. Tom Ren, 3. Maya Singh, 4. Chris Wu, 5. Dani Park, 6. Leo Tran, 7. Fatima Osei, 8. Sam Kim | Status: Confirmed
Sponsorship income 2025-2026: Alpha Consulting $500 (Silver), Campus Bank $1,000 (Gold), TechCorp $250 (Bronze). Total: $1,750. Payments received: all confirmed.
Gala ticket sales: 148 tickets sold at $25 each = $3,700. Student discounts applied: 12 tickets at $15 = $180. Total ticket revenue: $3,880.
Room bookings Q1 2026: Jan 14 — Room 204 (Workshop), Jan 28 — Atrium (Networking), Feb 7 — Atrium B (Speed Networking), Feb 14 — Room 302 (Workshop). All bookings confirmed via portal.
Name: Lily Zhang | Role: VP Communications | Email: lzhang@uni.edu | Year: 2nd | Responsible for: newsletter, social media, press releases
Category: Software | Description: Canva Pro annual subscription | Amount: $169.99 | Date: 2025-09-01 | Submitted by: VP Communications | Status: Paid via club card
March 2026 Events: Gala (Mar 28, The Grand Hall), Alumni Panel (Mar 12, Auditorium A), Budget Review Meeting (Mar 5, Room 101), Elections Info Session (Mar 19, online).
First-year members enrolled in mentorship program 2025-2026: 38 mentees matched with 38 mentors. Unmatched mentees waitlisted: 4. Program coordinator: VP Equity (Aisha Patel).
Equipment inventory: 1× portable projector (purchased Sep 2024, value $350), 2× HDMI adapters ($30 each), 1× laser pointer ($25), 1× portable speaker ($80). Storage: Exec Office, locker 7B.
Category: Decorations | Description: Winter Formal table centrepieces and string lights | Amount: $215 | Date: 2026-01-20 | Submitted by: VP Events | Status: Reimbursed
Spring Formal ticket sales: 95 tickets sold at $30 each = $2,850. 10 complimentary tickets for exec. Total attendees expected: 105. Revenue: $2,850.
Name: Aisha Patel | Role: VP Equity | Email: apatel@uni.edu | Year: 3rd | Responsible for: D&I committee, mentorship program, accessibility compliance
Hackathon 2026 registrations: 84 participants registered across 21 teams. University of Toronto: 34, McMaster: 20, York: 18, Other: 12. Registration closed Feb 15.
Category: Speaker Honorarium | Description: CFO Priya Singh — Finance Careers Night | Amount: $500 | Date: 2026-02-20 | Method: Student union cheque | Status: Processed
Post-event survey results — Winter Formal 2026: Overall rating 4.6/5 (n=89). Venue: 4.8/5. Food: 4.4/5. Music: 4.5/5. Top suggestion: earlier start time.
Name: Tom Ren | Role: VP Marketing | Email: tren@uni.edu | Year: 2nd | Responsible for: sponsorship outreach, brand partnerships, merchandise
Merchandise inventory — Fall 2025: 50 hoodies ordered, 43 sold at $35 each = $1,505. 7 remaining (S: 3, M: 2, L: 2). Profit after $10/unit cost: $25/unit × 43 = $1,075.
Category: Prizes | Description: Hackathon 2026 cash prizes (1st: $100, 2nd: $80, 3rd: $40, best presentation: $30) | Total: $250 | Date: 2026-02-28 | Status: Held in exec account
January 2026 Events: New Year Kickoff (Jan 10, Atrium), Budget Planning Meeting (Jan 14, Room 101), Resume Review Workshop (Jan 21, Room 204), Case Competition Info Night (Jan 28, online).
Name: David Park | Role: Director of Events | Email: dpark@uni.edu | Year: 2nd | Reports to: VP Events (Marcus Johnson) | Joined exec: September 2025

Catering vendor comparison: Campus Catering Co. $18/person (reliable, campus approved), Bite Catering $14/person (external, 2-week lead time), Feast & Co $22/person (premium, allergies accommodated). Recommended: Campus Catering Co.
